page.content: In the context of evaluating the physiological responses to saline infusion at a dosage of 40 mL/kg, the data presented in the accompanying table elucidate critical metrics related to movement and curvature over varying time intervals. Specifically, the metrics of "Move Distance (x, mm)" and "Curvature (×10−2 mm−1)" highlight the effects of saline administration on both the extent of movement and the associated curvature of the subjects over the first six hours post-infusion. The recorded move distance at the 1 h, 2 h, 4 h, and 6 h intervals indicates a progressive increase in movement, with values of 162 ± 28 mm at 1 hour and reaching 282 ± 68 mm by the 6-hour mark. This progressive increase may suggest an enhanced physiological response to the saline infusion over time. Furthermore, the curvature values, which reflect structural changes or adaptations in the subjects, demonstrate notable stability, with the curvature measured at 0.155 × 10−2 mm−1 at 1 hour and maintaining a similar trend throughout the observation period. This data underscores the dynamic interactions between saline infusion and the physiological parameters being monitored, providing insights into the temporal evolution of movement and curvature in response to fluid administration.
